item.title: Re: Site Crit - Rieview
item.content:encoded: I have to agree. Like, I understand some scips can get pretty dark, but there are reasons that it does. This… This just kinda feels like it was written just to be dark for dark's sake with no resolution to the story. It leaves way too many questions unanswered for such a simple scip, and I don't mean in a "It is a mystery" kind of way, more in a "full of plot holes" kind of way.

item.title: Re: Je me lance vers la gloire... okay
Site Crit - Rieview
item.content:encoded:
This is… really close to working. The tone is good, the length is exactly where it needs to be, and the story is… well, that's where it sort of falls apart for me.

There are two really big issues here. The first is that I'm really not sure why any of this is happening? Brigham gives up the ghost immediately and I don't know why. I also don't know why he did this. And not in a "oh man the human condition is scary" sort of way, the "wait, what?" kind of way.

It doesn't feel like a natural character consequence so much as "this needed to happen to get the kid to die". He casts this spell, which works and keeps her quiet… but then he kills her? Weird.

The second issue relates to the magic. I have no idea why this is happening. Again, he casts a spell, it doesn't entirely work, but then also it has this secondary fear causing effect. But there's no throughline, and the mention of "karmic spirits" just raises more questions than it answers - what are these? What are the rules? Why did they do this?

The pieces are all here, but the throughline isn't… quite, in a way that unfortunately detracts from the piece.

opinions my own, not of site staff, please contact SoullessSingularity if there's an issue

item.title: Re: Je me lance vers la gloire... okay
(no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, pretty good. When it brought up people freaking out in response to garbage trucks, I figured it was a joke SCP. Nice job throwing expectations.

item.title: Je me lance vers la gloire... okay
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this one's a tad on the grim side.

Thanks to Cerastes and Naveil for critique, among others (quite a few people read through this during the writing process).
